name: MIOSA Foundation
description: Component library & design system for MIOSA-powered applications

tokens.description: CSS custom properties required by all components

foundation.description: Design tokens and primitives — always include

primitives.description: Basic UI building blocks — always include

patterns.description: Reusable UI patterns — include as needed

modules.description: Full feature modules — pick based on platform features

ai.description: AI/Chat interface components — include if platform has AI

platform.description: Platform-level features — include for SaaS platforms

sections.name: Overview
sections.description: Library introduction, metrics, architecture overview

sections.description: Full terminal UI — window chrome with traffic lights, tab bars, split panes, agent CLI output with tool call cards, command output patterns (table / JSON / git log), and a settings panel with font, color scheme, cursor, and opacity controls.

moduleMap.description: Maps platform features to component sections. AI agents should read this to determine which sections to apply.

desktopShell.description: macOS-style desktop with dock, icons, windows

contentOS.description: Content management, CMS, blog, media, editorial

system.description: System-level settings, admin controls, feature flags

help.description: Help center, documentation, support tickets, guided tours

integrationHub.description: Integration management, webhooks, data sync, health monitoring

osa.description: OSA agent pill, floating AI assistant overlay

aiInstructions: To apply this component library to a new codebase: 1) Always include all 'foundation' and 'primitives' tier sections. 2) Read the moduleMap to match platform features to sections. 3) For each matching section, read the .svelte file to extract CSS patterns and HTML structure. 4) Each section uses a unique CSS prefix (cssPrefix field) to avoid conflicts. 5) All sections depend on the design token CSS custom properties defined in the tokens object. 6) Apply sections in tier order: foundation → primitives → patterns → modules → ai → platform.
